title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Alison Dumbie sends a letter to a recipient, expressing hope that they will be reunited with Charles or Giles soon and stating she will explain her own situation in a future correspondence.

In a separate setting, Miss Marple discusses a letter with Jules and Gwendolen. Jeeves reveals that his new home, Mount Edgecombe, is the site of a first-degree murder that occurred in the foyer approximately eighteen to twenty years ago. The victim was a beautiful woman whose only known detail is her Christian name. Jeeves expresses a desire to investigate the mystery, but Miss Marple becomes visibly distressed and warns the newlyweds to stay away from the matter. She argues that because the crime was never reported or suspected by the community, digging into the past could disrupt their peaceful lives and bring them pain. Despite her warnings, Jeeves remains determined to investigate the murder that happened in his own house.

Following her return to St. Mary Mead, Miss Marple appears absent-minded and exhausted, causing concern among her neighbors and her maid, Evelyn. She eventually summons her long-time friend and physician, Dr. Hedock. While the doctor attributes her weakness to the draining atmosphere of London, Miss Marple uses the pretext of needing restorative sea air to request a professional prescription for travel. She reveals her intention to go to the small town of Dartmouth, prompting Dr. Hedock to question her motive. Miss Marple responds by posing a hypothetical scenario regarding the discovery of an old, unreported murder.
